heading: General-use prepaid cards, gift certificates, and store gift cards
label: 1693l–1 General-use prepaid cards, gift certificates, and store gift cards
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior was renumbered section 916 and is classified to .section 915 of Pub. L. 90–321section 1693m of this title
Amendments2010— substituted “Bureau” for “Board” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 111–203

Effective Date of 2010 AmendmentAmendment by effective on the designated transfer date, see , set out as a note under , Government Organization and Employees.Pub. L. 111–203section 1100H of Pub. L. 111–203section 552a of Title 5

Effective Date, as added by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–24, title IV, § 403Pub. L. 111–209, § 1124 Stat. 2254July 27, 2010“(a).—In GeneralExcept as provided under subsection (b) of this section, this title [enacting this section and amending sections 1693m to 1693r of this title and provisions set out as a note under ] and the amendments made by this title shall become effective 15 months after the date of enactment of this Act [].section 1693 of this titleMay 22, 2009“(b)Exception.—“(1).—In generalIn the case of a gift certificate, store gift card, or general-use prepaid card that was produced prior to , the effective date of the disclosure requirements described in sections 915(b)(3) and (c)(2)(B) of the Electronic Funds [probably should be “Fund”] Transfer Act [–1(b)(3), (c)(2)(B)] shall be , provided that an issuer of such a certificate or card shall—April 1, 2010January 31, 201115 U.S.C. 1693l“(A)comply with paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 915(b) of such Act [–1(b)(1), (2)];15 U.S.C. 1693l“(B)consider any such certificate or card for which funds expire to have no expiration date with respect to the underlying funds;“(C)at a consumer’s request, replace such certificate or card that has funds remaining at no cost to the consumer; and“(D)comply with the disclosure requirements of paragraph (2) of this subsection.“(2).—Disclosure requirementsThe disclosure requirements of this subsection are met by providing notice to consumers, via in-store signage, messages during customer service calls, Web sites, and general advertising, that—“(A)any such certificate or card for which funds expire shall be deemed to have no expiration date with respect to the underlying funds;“(B)consumers holding such certificate or card shall have a right to a free replacement certificate or card that includes the packaging and materials, typically associated with such a certificate or card; and“(C)any dormancy fee, inactivity fee, or service fee for such certificates or cards that might otherwise be charged shall not be charged if such fees do not comply with section 915 of the Electronic Funds [probably should be “Fund”] Transfer Act [–1].15 U.S.C. 1693l“(3).—Period for disclosure requirementsThe notice requirements in paragraph (2) of this subsection shall continue until .”January 31, 2013, , , which provided that title IV of was to become effective 15 months after , was repealed by , , .Pub. children.heading: Dormancy fee; inactivity charge or fee
children.label: 1 Dormancy fee; inactivity charge or fee
children.text: The terms “dormancy fee” and “inactivity charge or fee” mean a fee, charge, or penalty for non-use or inactivity of a gift certificate, store gift card, or general-use prepaid card.

children.heading: General use prepaid card, gift certificate, and store gift card1So in original. Probably should be “General-use”.1
children.label: 2 General use prepaid card, gift certificate, and store gift card1So in original. Probably should be “General-use”.1
i
children.text: redeemable at multiple, unaffiliated merchants or service providers, or automated teller machines;

children.label: ii
children.text: issued in a requested amount, whether or not that amount may, at the option of the issuer, be increased in value or reloaded if requested by the holder;

children.label: iii
children.text: purchased or loaded on a prepaid basis; and

children.label: iv
children.text: honored, upon presentation, by merchants for goods or services, or at automated teller machines.

children.label: i
children.text: redeemable at a single merchant or an affiliated group of merchants that share the same name, mark, or logo;

children.label: ii
children.text: issued in a specified amount that may not be increased or reloaded;

children.label: iii
children.text: purchased on a prepaid basis in exchange for payment; and

children.label: iv
children.text: honored upon presentation by such single merchant or affiliated group of merchants for goods or services.

children.label: i
children.text: redeemable at a single merchant or an affiliated group of merchants that share the same name, mark, or logo;

children.label: ii
children.text: issued in a specified amount, whether or not that amount may be increased in value or reloaded at the request of the holder;

children.label: iii
children.text: purchased on a prepaid basis in exchange for payment; and

children.label: iv
children.text: honored upon presentation by such single merchant or affiliated group of merchants for goods or services.

children.label: i
children.text: used solely for telephone services;

children.label: ii
children.text: reloadable and not marketed or labeled as a gift card or gift certificate;

children.label: iii
children.text: a loyalty, award, or promotional gift card, as defined by the Bureau;

children.label: iv
children.text: not marketed to the general public;

children.label: v
children.text: issued in paper form only (including for tickets and events); or

children.label: I
children.text: at the event or venue after admission; or

children.label: II
children.text: in conjunction with admission to such events or venues, at specific locations affiliated with and in geographic proximity to the event or venue.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “service fee” means a periodic fee, charge, or penalty for holding or use of a gift certificate, store gift card, or general-use prepaid card.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: With respect to a general-use prepaid card, the term “service fee” does not include a one-time initial issuance fee.

children.heading: Prohibition on imposition of fees or charges
children.label: b Prohibition on imposition of fees or charges

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided under paragraphs (2) through (4), it shall be unlawful for any person to impose a dormancy fee, an inactivity charge or fee, or a service fee with respect to a gift certificate, store gift card, or general-use prepaid card.

children.label: A
children.text: there has been no activity with respect to the certificate or card in the 12-month period ending on the date on which the charge or fee is imposed;

children.label: B
children.text: the disclosure requirements of paragraph (3) have been met;

children.label: C
children.text: not more than one fee may be charged in any given month; and

children.label: D
children.text: any additional requirements that the Bureau may establish through rulemaking under subsection (d) have been met.

children.label: i
children.text: that a dormancy fee, inactivity charge or fee, or service fee may be charged;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such fee or charge;

children.label: iii
children.text: how often such fee or charge may be assessed; and

children.label: iv
children.text: that such fee or charge may be assessed for inactivity; and

children.label: B
children.text: the issuer or vendor of such certificate or card informs the purchaser of such charge or fee before such certificate or card is purchased, regardless of whether the certificate or card is purchased in person, over the Internet, or by telephone.

children.label: A
children.text: that is distributed pursuant to an award, loyalty, or promotional program, as defined by the Bureau; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to which, there is no money or other value exchanged.

children.heading: Prohibition on sale of gift cards with expiration dates
children.label: c Prohibition on sale of gift cards with expiration dates

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), it shall be unlawful for any person to sell or issue a gift certificate, store gift card, or general-use prepaid card that is subject to an expiration date.

children.label: A
children.text: the expiration date is not earlier than 5 years after the date on which the gift certificate was issued, or the date on which card funds were last loaded to a store gift card or general-use prepaid card; and

children.label: B
children.text: the terms of expiration are clearly and conspicuously stated.

children.label: A
children.text: prescribe regulations to carry out this section, in addition to any other rules or regulations required by this subchapter, including such additional requirements as appropriate relating to the amount of dormancy fees, inactivity charges or fees, or service fees that may be assessed and the amount of remaining value of a gift certificate, store gift card, or general-use prepaid card below which such charges or fees may be assessed; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall determine the extent to which the individual definitions and provisions of this subchapter or Regulation E should apply to general-use prepaid cards, gift certificates, and store gift cards.2So in original. The word “shall” probably should not appear.2

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In prescribing regulations under this subsection, the Bureau shall consult with the Federal Trade Commission.

children.heading: Timing; effective date
children.label: 3 Timing; effective date
children.text: The regulations required by this subsection shall be issued in final form not later than 9 months after .May 22, 2009
